full_text:
Monsoon gains momentum in Gujarat:Rain alert issued for 11 districts; warning issued to fishermen amid strong coastal winds
- Copy link
Light to moderate rainfall has been forecast in 11 districts of Gujarat today (June 28), while the Meteorological Department has issued a warning of heavy rain accompanied by thunderstorms and gusty winds in parts of South Gujarat from July 1.
During the last 24 hours, several parts of North Gujarat, including Sabarkantha, Mahisagar, Aravalli and Narmada districts, also received good rainfall.
While 54 talukas across the state are yet to receive any rain this monsoon, cities such as Ahmedabad and Gandhinagar continue to grapple with intense heat and humidity, with maximum temperatures remaining above the 40-degree Celsius mark.
Fishermen warned as coastal winds may touch 60 kmph
The Meteorological Department has issued a warning for fishermen on July 1, forecasting rough weather along the Gujarat coast with wind speeds of 40-50 kmph, gusting up to 60 kmph.
The southwest monsoon is expected to advance further into parts of the state over the next two to three days.
At present, a cyclonic circulation persists over and around North Gujarat, while a trough extending from Andhra Pradesh to Central Maharashtra continues to remain active.
Scattered showers likely across South Gujarat and Saurashtra
Light to moderate rain or thundershowers have been forecast at isolated places today in South Gujarat districts, including Vadodara, Chota Udaipur, Narmada, Surat, Dang, Tapi, Navsari and Valsad, besides Daman and Dadra & Nagar Haveli.
Similar weather is also expected in parts of Saurashtra, including Amreli, Bhavnagar, Gir Somnath and Diu. The remaining districts of Gujarat are likely to witness mainly dry weather.
Rain Forecast for Three Days from Today
Monsoon will gather momentum from the beginning of July
Mercury remains above 40°C despite monsoon arrival
Despite the onset of the monsoon in Gujarat, widespread rainfall is yet to set in across much of the state, with temperatures continuing to remain unusually high. On June 27, the maximum temperature was recorded at 39.9°C in Amreli, 37.6°C in Bhavnagar and 38.2°C in Bhuj.
Meanwhile, the mercury continued to stay above the 40°C mark in several parts of the state, including Ahmedabad, Gandhinagar, Rajkot and Surendranagar.
Rain activity likely to intensify across North, Central Gujarat and Saurashtra from July 2
According to weather analyst Jayprakash Madhak, “South Gujarat is likely to receive heavy rainfall on July 2 and 3. The southwest monsoon will gradually advance across different parts of Gujarat over the next four days and is expected to cover the entire state by July 2. A spell of widespread and good rainfall is likely across Gujarat between July 2 and July 7”.
As per Madhak's forecast, Saurashtra may continue to receive scattered light to moderate showers until July 1. Rainfall activity is expected to intensify across North Gujarat, Central Gujarat and Saurashtra from July 2 onwards. Bhavnagar district is also likely to receive good rainfall on July 1 and 2, while light to moderate showers are expected until then.
